# Wiregrid

> Industrial supervision (SCADA) platform built around two principles: no per-tag pricing, and data stored in a standard PostgreSQL database directly queryable from Power BI, Grafana or Excel.

Wiregrid acquires data from industrial PLCs over Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet/IP, exposes it live (sub-100 ms WebSocket push) and archives it into a TimescaleDB hypertable. Built for small-to-mid industrial sites (50–500 measurement points) where Niagara N4 and Ignition are too expensive and SaaS-only platforms are unacceptable for data residency.

## What Wiregrid is

- A web-based SCADA / IIoT supervision platform.
- Self-hosted via Docker Compose (single command).
- Backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + asyncpg + SQLAlchemy 2 (async).
- Database: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB.
- Frontend: Vue 3 + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Pinia (single-page app).
- Real-time: Redis pub/sub forwarded to browser via WebSocket.

## What Wiregrid is not

- Not a safety / SIL-rated control system. Use a certified PLC for interlocks. Wiregrid only supervises, alarms, archives.
- Not (yet) a wiresheet visual logic editor. The execution engine ships, the Vue Flow editor lands Q3 2026.
- Not a full HMI builder. No drag-drop screen designer (planned later).

## Capabilities shipped today (April 2026)

- Multi-protocol acquisition: Modbus TCP, OPC-UA, BACnet/IP.
- Live points (read + write) over WebSocket, polling fallback.
- TimescaleDB historian with per-point on_change deadband or fixed interval.
- Operational alarms: high/low thresholds with deadband and delay, severities (info/warning/critical), notification email groups, full event log.
- Git-style config versioning: every change goes into a revision. Diff between two revisions, single-transaction revert. Hot-reload via pg_notify.
- Open API: REST on /py/v1/{drivers,machines,points,alarms,revisions} and WebSocket /ws/live. Direct SQL access to PostgreSQL — Power BI connects via ODBC without a vendor connector.
- Multi-project / multi-tenant: project_id scoping on every entity.

## Differentiators vs Niagara N4 / Ignition / EcoStruxure

- Zero per-tag pricing. Niagara charges roughly €5/tag/year; Ignition's free tier ("Maker") has quota limits; EcoStruxure has per-device modules. Wiregrid Production tier is a flat €990/year for unlimited points on one instance.
- Data in standard PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB. No proprietary historian format. Power BI / Grafana / Metabase / Excel connect directly to your database — no export, no middleware, no licensed connector.
- Self-hosted by default. Your data never leaves your network. Runs on a Raspberry Pi 4, an industrial mini-PC, or a €5/month VPS. No JVM, no station application.
- Versioned config with one-click revert. SCADA platforms traditionally rely on manual backup; Wiregrid commits changes like a git repository and lets you roll back to any deployed revision in a single transaction.

## Hardware footprint

- Minimum: 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk.
- Tested on: Hetzner CX22, Raspberry Pi 4, industrial mini-PCs.
- Demo runs on a €4/month nano VPS.

## Pricing

- Discovery: €0/forever, up to 50 points, Modbus TCP only, 7-day historian, online docs support.
- Production: €990/year (excl. VAT), unlimited points, all 3 protocols, unlimited historian, email + video support.
- 12-month commitment. Self-hosted — Wiregrid has no access to your PostgreSQL.

## Live demo and signup

- Online demo: https://demo.wiregrid.fr (no signup, public, read-only-ish — you can edit but data resets periodically).
- Signup flow generates a private project clone in under a second (10 drivers, 13 machines, 18 alarms, 60 machine points pre-seeded).

## Open source

Not publicly yet. Candidate licenses at public release: AGPL-3.0 or source-available. In the meantime, all customer data is in standard SQL on the customer's own server — no lock-in even pre-OSS.

## Project status

Phases 1–6 complete (April 2026): Laravel-to-Python backend migration done, demo on Cloudflare Pages live, Hetzner-hosted interactive demo decommissioned. Wiresheet UI port (recovered from the deleted Laravel codebase) targeted for Q3 2026.
